facts.claim: Messaging: WebSocket+Firestore+Celery; Observacao auto-spawns Notificacao to all setor users. Counter at chats/{setor_id}/usuarios/{user_id}.qtd_mensagens: +1 new msg (sender excl.), -1 floor@0 when disengaged, ts-only on reaction.
Chat retention 48h default (96h w/ leito filter); emoji enum (7); one reaction/user via unique_together.
Video: Agora.io RTC+Firebase+mediaDevices gate; single-join guard; unconditional reload on leave; Firestore online_call roster flag.
Server always forces usuario_id/setor_id/responsavel_id/checado_por_id from auth context, never client body.

46 rules: data-validation 15, alert-threshold 9, care-pathway 12, scheduling-operational 9, triage-eligibility 1; OK 39, DISCREPANCY 5 (001,003,026,034,035), AMBIGUOUS 2 (041,045); NOT_APPLICABLE 43/UNVERIFIABLE 3 (001-003).
Unread-counter fanout hits every setor user, branching on is_reacao/reduzir_qtd/sender-exclusion; decrement fires only when the observation has 0 reactions, 0 checked checagens, 0 other replies.
At most one reaction per user per observation (unique_together); every save/delete re-triggers the Firebase counter.
A new Observacao auto-creates a Notificacao; tipo_mensagem='leito' if bed-bound else 'setor'.
Notificacao.save() bulk-creates one UsuarioNotificacao per setor user + dispatches a websocket task, first save only.
Chat retention is 48h default, 96h with a leito filter; boundary is exclusive (__lte excluded).
Reaction-count-by-emoji has two divergent impls: SQL GROUP BY (correct) vs Python groupby w/ no order_by (can undercount non-consecutive emoji).
Homecare bed-occupancy guard is entirely unenforced: validar_leito() is never called from save(); its inner !ocupado check is unreachable dead code anyway.

Other legacy/FE defects: acaoDict has 8 action keys (incl. 'editar') vs Feed.Acao TS union's 7, hidden by an 'as' cast (035); balanco_hidrico returns uncalled get_pk (003); feed hardcodes 4 NEUTRO mocks (026); video call-leave force-reloads, listener leaks (029/030).
Feeds v2 platform/experience/governance: chat+unread-badges, notification center, teleUTI video, activity feed, checklist audit.

PT-BR vocab (verbatim): emoji (7, BE=FE order) sorriso/choro/aprovacao/reprovacao/forca/gratidao/coracao; tipo_mensagem setor|leito; feed acao (FE, 8) criar/alterar/editar/inativar/assinar/liberar/administrar/nao_administrar.

constraints.text: usuario_id/setor_id/responsavel_id/checado_por_id MUST be server-derived, never accepted from client body.
A user MUST have at most one reaction per observation (unique_together enforced).
Reaction deletion MUST be permitted only for the reacting user's own reaction.
Unread-message counters MUST floor at 0 on decrement, never negative.
Chat retention MUST default to 48h, extending to 96h only when the query is scoped by leito.
A checagem checked by another user MUST NOT be re-toggled, nor toggled from a reply-preview.

open_questions: Reactions hard-delete (force_delete=True) vs the platform's soft-delete convention (027) - intentional, or align in v2?
Should v2 fix the reaction-count divergence platform-wide (001), or is groupby's behavior acceptable at current scale?
Is 'editar' a live feed action Feed.Acao must gain, or dead code in acaoDict that should be dropped (035)?
Should v2 enforce the unoccupied-bed guard for homecare feed actions that legacy code never enforced (034)?
